citations.claim: The provider agent runs on Mac hardware and serves local inference requests from the Darkbloom coordinator
Architecture includes hardware detection, model scanning, backend management, coordinator connection, and Secure Enclave attestation
WebSocket client manages provider's connection to coordinator with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, and inference request handling
Security module implements runtime protections including PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping to prevent inference data inspection
Hardware detection queries macOS system APIs via sysctl and system_profiler to detect Apple Silicon capabilities and memory bandwidth
Model management scans HuggingFace cache for MLX models and performs SHA-256 weight fingerprinting for attestation
Ephemeral X25519 key pair generated fresh on every provider launch, protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH + Hardened Runtime + SIP
In-process inference engine using embedded Python runs inference inside hardened Rust process with no IPC channel to sniff
Backend management wraps inference engines with health monitoring and automatic restart using exponential backoff
Telemetry architecture includes tracing layer, panic hook, stderr scraper feeding into mpsc queue and async batcher for HTTPS transmission
Service management integrates with launchd as user agent, only running when user explicitly starts via darkbloom start
Configuration system stores TOML format at ~/.config/eigeninference/provider.toml with hardware-adapted defaults
External dependencies include tokio for async runtime, reqwest for HTTP, crypto_box for NaCl encryption, and pyo3 for Python embedding
Component connects to coordinator via WebSocket and downloads models from R2 CDN with configurable URLs
PT_DENY_ATTACH syscall prevents debugger attachment and makes process memory unreadable when combined with SIP and Hardened Runtime
Service serves inference requests in main event loop with security verification, backend management, and coordinator communication
Python path locking restricts imports to only approved runtime roots preventing provider from injecting malicious code
Provider-coordinator protocol includes Registration, Heartbeat, InferenceRequest, and AttestationChallenge message types over WebSocket
